title: Do I Wanna Know?
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Have you got colour in your cheeks?
  > [#1, 317 votes] Blushing is one of the many involuntary body signals that is caused by blood rushing to the cheeks. This is usually caused by embarrassment (due to adrenaline) or sexual excitement (due to hormones)...
Do you ever get that fear that you can't shift the type
  > [#2, 259 votes] “Summat” is Yorkshire slang for “something.” By asking if she worries about getting rid of something, Alex may be showing paranoia about him being the unwanted thing that’s sticking around like a...
That sticks around like summat in your teeth?
Are there some aces up your sleeve?
  > [#3, 150 votes] The idiom “having an ace up your sleeve” means to have an advantage that other people don’t know about. In this case, Alex feels like she’s keeping something from him or has an advantage over him...
Have you no idea that you're in deep?
I've dreamt about you nearly every night this week
  > [#4, 135 votes] Freud theorized that the content of dreams were dictated by unconscious wish fulfillment. Alex is plagued by the desire to see his lover, and thus this is manifested in his dreams. First in this song...
How many secrets can you keep?
'Cause there's this tune I found
  > [#5, 239 votes] Memories are triggered by scents or sounds that were present during the event being remembered and/or the content of the song being played. Alex finds a song that reminds him of his lover, and in his...
That makes me think of you somehow an' I play it on repeat
Until I fall asleep, spillin' drinks on my settee
  > [#6, 232 votes] A “settee” is an old English term for a sofa/couch, often seated for two. Further proof that this girl is in deep in this relationship with him–he’s drinking alone and falls asleep while obsessively...

[Pre-Chorus]
(Do I wanna know) If this feelin' flows both ways?
  > [#7, 273 votes] The several questions of the first verse, if answered, would tell him whether or not his lover is interested in him. He shows some anxiety about knowing the answers, indicating that he isn’t very...
(Sad to see you go) Was sorta hopin' that you'd stay
(Baby, we both know) That the nights were mainly made
  > [#8, 233 votes] Studies have shown what we’ve known for a while: alcohol makes you do stupid things. Alcohol is an inhibitor of the prefrontal cortex, which is in charge of processing information that “reflect both...
For sayin' things that you can't say tomorrow day

[Chorus]
Crawlin' back to you
  > [#9, 248 votes] The word “crawling” highlights his desperate and vulnerable condition–he’s on his knees and can’t think of anyone else but his lover, despite the pain it’s been causing. Alex mentions his unfortunate...
Ever thought of callin' when
You've had a few?
'Cause I always do
Maybe I'm too
  > [#10, 130 votes] Rebounds are nothing new–getting over one person by having sex with someone else is an often promoted remedy to those lingering feelings of an ended relationship. Rebound relationships in this sense...
Busy bein' yours
To fall for somebody new
Now I've thought it through
Crawlin' back to you

[Verse 2]
So have you got the guts?
  > [#11, 94 votes] After trying a few different tactics to get her to stay, like accusatory questions and subtle pleas, he now tries daring her to come back to him. This is a technique of reverse psychology, in which...
Been wonderin' if your heart's still open
  > [#12, 128 votes] He plays on the idea of her heart as some sort of store or service that can be open or closed for him. Not only does he want to know if there’s a vacancy left for him, he wants to know how long it...
And if so, I wanna know what time it shuts
Simmer down an' pucker up, I'm sorry to interrupt
  > [#13, 122 votes] Rather than hearing out what she’s trying to say, he interrupts her with an attempted kiss – seemingly as a last resort of resparking her interest in the relationship. Also note the play on the...
It's just I'm constantly on the cusp of tryin' to kiss you
But I don't know if you feel the same as I do
  > [#14, 111 votes] He’s not sure if the feelings between them are mutual, but his offer to be together is on the table if she does want it. There’s also an inability to move on throughout this song, hence “crawling...
But we could be together if you wanted to

[Pre-Chorus]
(Do I wanna know) If this feelin' flows both ways?
  > [#7, see above]
(Sad to see you go) Was sorta hopin' that you'd stay
(Baby, we both know) That the nights were mainly made
  > [#8, see above]
For sayin' things that you can't say tomorrow day

[Chorus]
Crawlin' back to you (Crawlin' back to you)
  > [#9, see above]
Ever thought of callin' when
You've had a few? (You've had a few)
'Cause I always do ('Cause I always do)
Maybe I'm too (Maybe I'm too busy)
  > [#10, see above]
Busy bein' yours (Bein' yours)
To fall for somebody new
Now I've thought it through
Crawlin' back to you

[Outro]
(Do I wanna know) If this feelin' flows both ways?
  > [#7, see above]
(Sad to see you go) Was sorta hopin' that you'd stay
(Baby, we both know) That the nights were mainly made
  > [#8, see above]
For sayin' things that you can't say tomorrow day
(Do I wanna know?) Too busy bein' yours to fall
  > [#10, see above]
(Sad to see you go) Ever thought of callin', darlin'?
  > [#9, see above]
(Do I wanna know?) Do you want me crawlin' back to you?
  > [#15, 160 votes] The final line underlines his insecurity. He can’t move on, so he’s crawling back. However, he isn’t certain that she actually wants him back. This uncertainty itself is an unpleasant feeling, but...
